fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Mississippi Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Mississippi - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity mississippi, how to transfer dietitian license to mississippi, dietitian compact mississippi, mississippi dietitian endorsement application

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Every 2 years (by March 31 of odd years) + 30 CE hours

license_types.title: Licensed Dietitian (LD)
license_types.description: The credential to practice dietetics in Mississippi, overseen by the Mississippi State Department of Health.

license_types.title: Provisional Licensed Dietitian
license_types.description: Available for those who have completed their education and supervised practice but have not yet passed the CDR exam. Valid for one year, costs $50, and requires practice under the supervision of a Licensed Dietitian.

steps.title: Complete Education & Supervised Practice
steps.description: Earn a master's degree or higher from an ACEND-accredited program. Complete a minimum of 900 hours of board-approved supervised practice through an ACEND-accredited dietitian internship.

steps.title: Pass the CDR Examination
steps.description: Successfully pass the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) Registration Examination for Dietitians. Verification of current RD status is required.

steps.title: Pass the Jurisprudence Exam
steps.description: All applicants must successfully complete an examination testing their knowledge of Mississippi's dietetic statutes and administrative rules.

steps.title: Submit the Application via LEMS
steps.description: Create an account on Mississippi's Licensure and Enforcement Management System (LEMS) portal. Complete the application and pay the $100 fee.

documents.detail: Proof of active registration with the Commission on Dietetic Registration.
Documentation of successfully passing the state-specific rules exam.
Sent directly from the university showing your conferred degree.

provisional_text: Mississippi offers a provisional license ($50 fee) for individuals who have completed the academic and supervised practice requirements but have not yet passed the CDR exam. A Provisional Licensed Dietitian must practice under the direct supervision of an active Mississippi Licensed Dietitian. The provisional license is valid for one year and can be renewed annually for an additional $50.
reciprocity_text: Mississippi allows for licensure by reciprocity (endorsement) for applicants holding a current, active license in another state with requirements substantially equivalent to Mississippi's, provided the out-of-state license is in good standing.

faqs.question: How much does a Mississippi dietitian license cost?
faqs.answer: The initial license application fee is a non-refundable $100. The provisional license costs $50.

faqs.question: When does the Mississippi license need to be renewed?
faqs.answer: Mississippi regular dietitian licenses are renewed biennially (every two years) by March 31 of odd-numbered years. The renewal fee is $100.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have state-specific continuing education requirements?
faqs.answer: Yes. Regular license holders must complete 30 hours of approved continuing education every two years. Provisional license holders must complete 15 hours annually.

faqs.question: Does Mississippi have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Mississippi. To work in Mississippi, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Mississippi?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 3 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Mississippi while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: A temporary or provisional credential is available while the full endorsement application is pending.

reciprocity.notes: Mississippi allows for licensure by reciprocity (endorsement) for applicants holding a current, active license in another state with requirements substantially equivalent to Mississippi's, provided the out-of-state license is in good standing.
